what_it_does: An acceptance test script that exercises runtime.session_scan.resolve_own_session to verify it never silently mis-identifies the current session when multiple transcript files exist and no explicit identity is supplied.
observations: Monkeypatches ss.PROJECTS_DIR to a temporary directory and uses ss._encode_cwd to build a fake project directory.
Creates one or two .jsonl transcript files and calls resolve_own_session with cwd, session_id, allow_ambiguous, and COMPANY_SESSION_ID variants.
Expects AmbiguousSelfError when two transcripts exist without an explicit session ID.
Expects env COMPANY_SESSION_ID and explicit session_id to resolve unambiguously.
Expects allow_ambiguous=True to return a best-guess result flagged ambiguous.
Expects FileNotFoundError when the env COMPANY_SESSION_ID points to a non-existent transcript.
Uses a hand-rolled ok() helper and PASS/FAIL lists instead of a test framework.
Sleeps 0.01 seconds to ensure distinct mtimes for newest-mtime guessing.
standouts: Frames the test as a FALSIFY-FIRST safety keystone.
Treats AmbiguousSelfError as the critical anti-hazard behavior.
Identifies COMPANY_SESSION_ID as the injection seam for unambiguous self-identification.
conventions: Manual pass/fail reporting and sys.exit(1) on failure.
concerns: Mutates global module state (ss.PROJECTS_DIR) and os.environ without isolation or restoration of the module constant.
Uses open() without a context manager.
Sleeps in a test to manipulate mtime.
Relies on internal _encode_cwd helper, coupling the test to implementation details.
notes: A safety acceptance test that treats loud failure on ambiguous self-resolution as the primary correctness property.
questions: What exact keys and value types does resolve_own_session return in every branch?
How is AmbiguousSelfError defined and what guarantees its message contains "WRONG self"?
What is the default value of allow_ambiguous in resolve_own_session?
Does _encode_cwd handle all filesystem-safe transformations consistently?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual test cases: it verifies unambiguous resolution paths and loud failure on ambiguity.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make resolve_own_session's safety contract explicit: implicit newest-mtime guessing is hazardous and must be opt-in only; explicit identity (env var or argument) is the normal, safe path.
novelty: The FALSIFY-FIRST narrative and the framing of a single acceptance test as the 'keystone' for self-serve memory safety.
contribution: A behavioral contract test that documents and guards the resolve_own_session resolution rules across single-transcript, ambiguous-no-id, env-ID, explicit-ID, allow-ambiguous, and missing-env-transcript cases.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying resolve_own_session raises on ambiguous directories lacking explicit identity, resolves via env or explicit session ID, and returns a flagged best-guess only when allow_ambiguous is true.
intention_for_embedding: To act as a safety keystone ensuring session self-resolution fails loudly rather than silently misidentifying the current session.

symbols.does: Records a test result as passed or failed and prints a concise status line.
symbols.description: The ok helper appends the test name to PASS or FAIL based on the cond boolean, prints an ok/FAIL line, and includes the detail string only on failure. It provides the manual pass/fail bookkeeping used by the acceptance script.
